title: Cleomella lutea
taxonomy: Cleomella lutea is a member of the family Cleomaceae and is classified as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Brassicales, and family Cleomaceae. It belongs to the genus Cleomella and is further classified into the species C. lutea. The classification of this plant is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics. Cleomella lutea is closely related to other species in the Cleomella genus, which is part of the larger family Cleomaceae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships among different species within the Cleomella genus.
uses: Cleomella lutea, also known as the yellow beeplant, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help treat wounds and skin infections. Cleomella lutea is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating fungal infections and promoting wound healing. Additionally, the plant's roots are used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cleomella lutea for medicinal purposes.

intro: Cleomella lutea, also known as Yellow Beeplant, is an annual herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Cleomella lutea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat minor skin issues and promote wound healing.
